open_bugs.title: Reports loadReports throws Firestore INTERNAL ASSERTION FAILED and cascades (poisons whole Firestore client for the session)
open_bugs.repro: Insights -> Reports. Dashboard renders but console: '[Reports] loadReports failed: FIRESTORE (10.12.2) INTERNAL ASSERTION FAILED: Unexpected state' (x2). Then subsequent Firestore ops on the same page-session also fail (loadNotifications onSnapshot crm-snooze.js:91), recurs across SPA hash routes until a full reload. Page rendering survives (uses preloaded window._leads) so it's silent.

gotchas: Brave Shields block cross-origin iframe embeds (Firebase Storage, cal.com, e-sign): iframe fires 'load' not 'error'; detect via contentWindow.location.href probe (SecurityError=success, readable value=blocked). Brave also blocks Esri tiles AND can render the dashboard sidebar invisibly (M-3). Drive QA in Chrome; do a SEPARATE Brave pass for render bugs the Chrome-driven sweep cannot see.

Esri tiles (server.arcgisonline.com) are blocked by Brave Shields -> Leaflet map renders as a black void. Default new maps to Google mt{s}.google.com primary with Esri tileerror fallback. Blank map tiles in QA are usually this, not a real bug.

App's OWN CSP frame-src governs portal/doc inline previews (firebase.json, per /pro page) — was missing entirely (fell back to default-src 'self') so cross-origin Storage URLs were blocked in EVERY browser, not just Brave. M-5 fix added Storage origins; verify after a hard-refresh (CSP headers cache).

NBDDocViewer/estimate doc preview: NEVER set a cross-origin Storage PDF as iframe.src — Chrome/Brave block it AND it FREEZES the CDP/print renderer (froze the screenshot tool repeatedly). Preview must be same-origin srcdoc HTML; server PDF -> Download/new-tab only.

Print-based doc render froze the browser-tool screenshot mid-session (recovered on navigate). To verify rendered doc output, render to srcdoc and screenshot that, or have Jo eyeball it — don't rely on CDP screenshot of the print preview.
admin.firestore.FieldValue is undefined in the emulator runtime — use the modular 'firebase-admin/firestore' import (FieldValue from there).

Emulator secrets load from functions/.env.local (NOT .env). Run the emulator on a demo- project: a real project pulls prod Secret Manager stubs via ADC = a RULE-0 prod-data leak. 8 emulator-backed specs need vendor API keys in functions/.env.local for AI/billing/SMS/e-sign paths.

serviceAccountTokenCreator IAM gap: ALL access-code logins (demo + member) are broken in prod because the function compute SA lacks roles/iam.serviceAccountTokenCreator -> createCustomToken fails. Fix is an IAM grant Jo/devops must run (access-control change is outside QA's safe-action scope; Claude must NOT run it). See estimate-remediation-2026-06-09/IAM-RUNBOOK.md.

onRepSignup blocking claim-setter is UNDEPLOYED (GCIP gap, in NBD_DEPLOY_SKIP_LIST). New reps/tenants will have claim-less tokens -> any companyId-gated rule denies their writes (root cause of H-5/H-2). JD's claims were backfilled manually via functions/set-jd-claims.js; a fresh signup will hit the same wall until onRepSignup deploys or rules reconcile to isOwner.

Deployed firestore.rules can be STALE vs repo: the leads update/delete rule in the repo is isOwner-based (would allow a claim-less owner), yet prod denied owner writes — meaning deployed != repo. Re-deploy current rules. Every push to main auto-deploys (rules gated on the cross-tenant emulator test).

Every push to main auto-deploys to Firebase prod via firebase-deploy.yml (~9 min: rules->storage->indexes->functions->hosting). 'Merged' ~= 'deployed' — do not assume on-main work is undeployed. JS/CSS served with max-age=300 (cache-busted fetch to confirm a live deploy); CSP headers cache too (hard-refresh).

Stripe is treated as LIVE in QA — drive to the pre-submit boundary, never submit. Test users password is test1234. Use ZZ_QA_ prefix for all test leads/jobs; email to jonathandeal459@gmail.com; never touch real customer records (Sherrill/McCain).

Estimate config-unification is silently defeated for any user who ever SAVED estimate settings: localStorage nbd_est_settings_v2 snapshot overrode unified config (L-1 root). Fix shipped; watch for the same pattern on any other localStorage-snapshotted config.

Firestore WebChannel 503 storm on dashboard load (~70 of 107 requests) is BENIGN stream-cycling, not a failure (writes persist) — do not chase it. Separately, the Firestore 'INTERNAL ASSERTION FAILED: Unexpected state' from Reports (H-3) is REAL and poisons the client for the whole hash-routed session until a full reload.
Test browser had Grammarly + Adobe Acrobat extensions injecting benign console noise and one popup interrupted a save — use a clean/incognito profile to isolate any save/click bug.

Photo upload could NOT be tested via the browser file_upload tool (rejects generated images — only accepts user-shared files). AI classify + photo/inspection report are unverified end-to-end; needs Jo to drag-drop or share a real test photo.

No CSP-blocked inline onclick/onchange handlers on /pro pages — the app routes UI actions through a data-action=call delegate gated by _NBD_CALL_ALLOWLIST; a missing allowlist entry = a silently dead button (root of C-1 + H-4). When auditing a non-working button, check the allowlist first.
known_blockers: serviceAccountTokenCreator IAM grant (devops-only, Jo/Claude must NOT run) — blocks all access-code/demo + member logins in prod.
onRepSignup claim-setter undeployed (GCIP upgrade gap) — new reps/tenants get claim-less tokens; companyId-gated writes denied until deployed or rules reconciled.
Per-seat billing ($39/seat Crew) not built (Phase D) — gates public self-serve launch + PR #579; needs Stripe test-mode keys + amounts + Firestore backup before build.
Crew price $299 (marketing) vs $249 (live Stripe STRIPE_PRICE_PROFESSIONAL) — product decision pending Jo; blocks PR #579 merge.
8 emulator-backed test specs + AI/billing/SMS/e-sign emulator paths blocked without vendor API keys in functions/.env.local; run only on a demo- project.
Brave-specific render bugs (sidebar invisible M-3, Esri tiles, iframe embeds) cannot be caught by the Chrome-driven sweep — a dedicated Brave pass is its own gated effort.
Photo upload + AI classify end-to-end cannot be driven by the browser file_upload tool — needs a user-shared/drag-dropped real image.
